title: Create Agenda package scaffolding and storage facade module
goal: Introduce a new package boundary for Agenda with an import-safe public storage facade before any task, DAG, or log behavior is implemented.
context: Stage 3 is the Agenda stage that turns canonical deliverables into executable tasks with durable work evidence.
The live repository already has split engine boundaries for Scintilla, Finis, and Formam under azq/.
Wave A is the storage-and-schema foundation wave for Agenda, not a command-implementation wave.
The Stage 3 build checklist says Agenda should start with package scaffolding and a thin storage facade rather than a monolithic implementation blob.
requirements: Create the azq/agenda package.
Create azq/agenda/storage.py as the public Stage 3 storage boundary.
Keep the initial modules import-safe and small.
Do not add azq task or azq dag command handling yet.
Do not introduce task generation, DAG building, or task state transitions in this task.
acceptance_criteria: azq/agenda/__init__.py exists.
azq/agenda/storage.py exists.
The new Agenda storage facade can be imported without error.

title: Extract Agenda path constants and directory helpers into a focused module
goal: Centralize Agenda path ownership for tasks, DAGs, and logs in a dedicated module before storage helpers start reading or writing files.
context: The live Stage 2 Formam code already split path ownership into azq/formam/paths.py rather than burying it in a giant storage module.
Stage 3 requires three canonical storage surfaces: data/agenda/tasks/, data/agenda/dags/, and data/agenda/logs/.
Wave A should establish deterministic file-path conventions before any Agenda command-layer work exists.
requirements: Create a dedicated azq/agenda/paths.py module.
Define constants for the tasks, dags, and logs directories.
Add helpers to ensure all three directories exist.
Add a helper to map a task_id like TASK_001 to a canonical task file path.
Add a helper to map a goal_id like FINIS_001 to a canonical DAG file path such as GOAL_FINIS_001_DAG.json.
Add a helper to map a task_id like TASK_001 to a canonical log file path such as TASK_001_LOG.md.
Add a helper to list task files in deterministic order.
Expose the path helpers through azq/agenda/storage.py only as a thin facade if needed.
acceptance_criteria: One focused module owns the canonical Agenda path decisions.
Agenda directories can be created on demand.
Task, DAG, and log path lookup is deterministic.
suggested_commit_message: add agenda path constants and helpers

title: Extract canonical task schema helpers into a focused module
goal: Create a dedicated schema module that normalizes partial task-shaped data into the Stage 3 canonical task record before any file format or repository helpers depend on it.
context: The Stage 3 build checklist defines the canonical task fields as task_id, deliverable_id, description, dependencies, status, execution_notes, and created.
The live Stage 2 refactor pattern already uses a dedicated schemas module for Formam normalization.
Agenda tasks must remain subordinate to deliverables rather than becoming free-floating work records.
requirements: Add task normalization helpers in azq/agenda/schemas.py.
Ensure every normalized task record includes task_id, deliverable_id, description, dependencies, status, execution_notes, and created.
Provide conservative fallback values for missing dependencies, status, execution_notes, and created fields.
Normalize dependencies into a list.
Keep the default status simple and explicit for Stage 3 foundation work.
Expose the normalization helper through azq/agenda/storage.py only as a thin facade if needed.
acceptance_criteria: One focused module can normalize incomplete task-shaped data into canonical task records.
Canonical task output always includes the Stage 3 required fields.
Dependencies always normalize to a list.

title: Implement task storage helpers in a dedicated task-storage module
goal: Make canonical task records readable, writable, listable, and allocatable through a dedicated task-storage module rather than growing the Agenda facade into a grab bag.
context: The Stage 3 build checklist says task storage should live in a dedicated task-storage module.
Stage 3 task files are the first visible Agenda artifacts and should be diff-friendly and inspectable.
Wave A must establish file-backed task reads and id allocation before any Wave B command work can create or mutate tasks.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/task_storage.py.
Choose and implement a diff-friendly markdown on-disk format for TASK_###.md files.
Add serializer and parser helpers for canonical task records.
Add repository helpers to load all tasks, load one task by exact task_id, load all tasks for one exact deliverable_id, and compute the next stable TASK_### id.
Keep ordering deterministic and lookup exact-match only.
Expose the task storage helpers through azq/agenda/storage.py only as a thin facade if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Task records can be normalized, written, read, listed, and allocated through a dedicated task-storage module.
The resulting task files are human-readable and diff-friendly.
The Agenda storage facade does not need to own the full implementation directly.

title: Add canonical deliverable-parent validation helpers for Agenda tasks
goal: Ensure Agenda can prove a task descends from a real canonical deliverable before any task file is written or mutated.
context: Stage 3 tasks must descend from canonical Formam deliverables, not from goals directly and not from hidden state.
The Stage 3 build checklist calls for one exact-match deliverable validation boundary before tasks are created or mutated.
The live repo already has canonical Formam deliverable storage under azq/formam/.
requirements: Add a validation helper that loads one canonical deliverable by exact deliverable_id.
Reject missing parent deliverables before any Agenda task write occurs.
Fail clearly when a deliverable record is malformed enough that task derivation should stop.
Keep deliverable validation logic small and reusable by later Agenda commands.
Do not broaden this task into DAG creation or task state transition behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda exposes a deliverable validation helper.
The helper checks parent deliverables by exact deliverable_id.
A future Wave B command can use the helper to stop invalid task creation early.

title: Extract canonical DAG schema helpers into the shared schema module
goal: Extend the Agenda schema boundary so partial DAG-shaped data can normalize into the canonical Stage 3 DAG record before DAG storage helpers are implemented.
context: The Stage 3 build checklist defines the canonical DAG fields as goal_id, deliverable_ids, task_ids, dependency_edges, status, created, and notes.
Wave A should define both task and DAG schema boundaries before any command layer starts generating executable structures.
The first DAG implementation should stay sparse and inspectable rather than pretending to have smart planning already.
requirements: Add DAG normalization helpers in azq/agenda/schemas.py.
Ensure every normalized DAG record includes goal_id, deliverable_ids, task_ids, dependency_edges, status, created, and notes.
Provide conservative fallback values for missing generated fields.
Normalize deliverable_ids, task_ids, and dependency_edges into lists.
Keep the default status simple and explicit.
Expose the normalization helper through azq/agenda/storage.py only as a thin facade if needed.
acceptance_criteria: One focused module can normalize incomplete DAG-shaped data into canonical DAG records.
Canonical DAG output always includes the Stage 3 required fields.
deliverable_ids, task_ids, and dependency_edges always normalize to lists.

title: Implement DAG storage helpers in a dedicated dag-storage module
goal: Make canonical DAG records readable, writable, and inspectable through a dedicated DAG-storage module so graph storage does not get buried in a generic Agenda facade.
context: The Stage 3 build checklist calls for a dedicated DAG-storage module rather than a monolithic storage.py.
Stage 3 DAG files should be simple, inspectable JSON artifacts under data/agenda/dags/.
Wave A should establish a canonical DAG representation before Wave B command work starts building or showing DAGs.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/dag_storage.py.
Choose and implement a simple JSON on-disk format for GOAL_<goal_id>_DAG.json files.
Add serializer and parser helpers for canonical DAG records.
Add repository helpers to load one DAG by exact goal_id and write one canonical DAG record.
Add a helper to derive stable dependency-edge structures from task relationships, keeping that behavior beside DAG logic rather than in a generic facade.
Expose the DAG storage helpers through azq/agenda/storage.py only as a thin facade if needed.
acceptance_criteria: DAG records can be normalized, written, read, and inspected through a dedicated DAG-storage module.
Dependency-edge derivation has a clear home beside DAG logic.
The Agenda storage facade remains smaller than the full implementation.

title: Implement task-log helpers in a dedicated log-storage module
goal: Create the append-only log-storage boundary that will let Agenda leave durable work evidence without leaking into artifact publication or archive behavior.
context: Stage 3 requires durable work-log evidence under data/agenda/logs/ whenever a task starts or completes.
The first log implementation should be append-only, human-readable, and clearly separate from later artifact or archive work.
Wave A should establish the log-writing boundary before any Wave B task command tries to use it.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/log_storage.py.
Add helpers to create one task log file for a canonical task.
Add helpers to append a visible started entry and a visible completed entry.
Keep the log format human-readable and append-only.
Keep log writing separate from artifact publication, archive behavior, and repository-wide status logic.
Expose the log helpers through azq/agenda/storage.py only as a thin facade if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda can leave durable task-log evidence through a dedicated log-storage module.
The log format is readable and inspectable by a human operator.
No later-stage artifact or archive behavior is introduced.

title: Keep azq agenda storage as a thin public facade
goal: Establish azq/agenda/storage.py as the stable public boundary that re-exports Agenda storage helpers from focused modules without becoming the implementation blob itself.
context: The live Stage 2 Formam code already uses a thin storage facade over focused modules such as paths.py, schemas.py, deliverable_storage.py, and map_storage.py.
The Stage 3 build checklist explicitly says azq/agenda/storage.py should remain the public boundary rather than becoming a monolithic implementation file that needs later cleanup.
Wave A closes when the Agenda foundation exists as a clean storage surface ready for Wave B command work.
requirements: Re-export the public Agenda storage helpers from the focused modules through azq/agenda/storage.py.
Keep the facade readable, stable, and import-safe for later command-layer modules.
Avoid duplicating implementation logic in the facade when the focused modules already own the real behavior.
Update azq/agenda/__init__.py only as needed to present a clean package boundary.
Do not introduce azq task or azq dag CLI command handling in this task.
acceptance_criteria: azq/agenda/storage.py is the public Agenda storage boundary, not the implementation blob.
The focused modules own paths, schemas, task storage, DAG storage, and log storage concerns.
Wave B command modules can depend on a stable Agenda storage facade without reopening Wave A boundaries.
suggested_commit_message: keep agenda storage as thin facade
